steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a small cross — one vertical line and one horizontal line that meet at the center of where your ring will sit. This axis keeps the ring from drifting lopsided.

steps.title: Block the outer circle (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a loose circle around your cross. A perfect circle isn't the goal — feel out the cleanest path for your ink stroke to follow.

steps.title: Block the inner hole (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a smaller circle inside the first, centered on the same axis. Leave a chunky band of space between them — life preservers are thick, not thin.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace both circles
steps.description: Switch to ink. Lay down the outer circle in one committed pass, then the inner circle in another. Don't worry about matching the pencil exactly — your hand will pick the cleanest line.

steps.title: Ink the rope wraps (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, add four short straight marks across the ring — at top, bottom, left, and right. These suggest the rope bindings that hold the ring's cover in place.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove the axis cross and any pencil construction. Leave faint pencil only where it reads as nice texture.
